records.ety: [L. imitatus, p. p. of imitari to imitate; of unknown origin. Cf. Image.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Imitated (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Imitating (?).]

defs: To follow as a pattern, model, or example; to copy or strive to copy, in acts, manners etc.
quotes: Despise wealth and imitate a dog.

defs: To produce a semblance or likeness of, in form, character, color, qualities, conduct, manners, and the like; to counterfeit; to copy.
quotes: A place picked out by choice of best alive The Nature's work by art can imitate.
This hand appeared a shining sword to weild, And that sustained an imitated shield.

defs: To resemble (another species of animal, or a plant, or inanimate object) in form, color, ornamentation, or instinctive habits, so as to derive an advantage thereby; as, when a harmless snake imitates a venomous one in color and manner, or when an odorless insect imitates, in color, one having secretion offensive to birds.
